fr.elements: Main Building; Vehicle Parking Area; Landscaped Area
fr.recommendations: Ensuring that multiple access points are clearly marked and maintaining unobstructed routes through the property could facilitate prompt emergency responder arrival and streamline movement during critical situations. To mitigate vehicle congestion, organizing parking in a manner that preserves clear travel lanes and staging areas may support quick ingress and egress. Thoughtful management of the landscaped areas to prevent overgrowth near access routes and parking zones can additionally improve visibility and allow for easier navigation of emergency vehicles and personnel, enhancing overall operational efficacy on the property.

wildfire.conditions: Tree Cluster; Limited Defensible Space; Vegetation Adjacent to Structure
wildfire.recommendations: Expanding defensible space by increasing the separation between tree clusters and structures could enhance property preparedness. Reducing vegetation continuity and density immediately adjacent to the building may lower the potential exposure to radiated heat and direct flames. Managing and thinning dense vegetation concentrations within proximity to the structure would improve safety zones for suppression operations and support long-term wildfire resilience efforts. Ensuring open areas around the property are well-maintained and unobstructed can better support firefighter access and reduce potential fire spread opportunities. Efforts towards these measures may further enhance defensible space, providing improved preparedness against wildfire impacts.
